Part I — Detailed findings translated into Blender decisions
Finding 1 — A car paint is not adequately represented by one homogeneous BRDF
Finding 2 — Sparkle is a spatial and angular high-frequency phenomenon
Finding 3 — Pixel footprint determines whether flakes should be discrete
Finding 4 — Flake orientation distribution matters more than a random normal strength
Finding 5 — Flake morphology changes the lobe and should not be hidden inside density
Finding 6 — Clearcoat is a vertical dielectric layer, not another shader added on top
Finding 7 — Coat and base require separate normal maps
Finding 8 — Orange peel is multiband surface waviness, not random micro-roughness
Finding 9 — Gloss, haze and DOI are different controls
Finding 10 — Metallic face/flop is driven by effect-pigment statistics, not clearcoat Fresnel
Finding 11 — Pearlescence and interference colour travel are not coloured metallic flakes
Finding 12 — Direct-light sparkle and diffuse-light graininess are separate perceptual attributes
Finding 13 — Flake size and area weighting affect both sparkle character and average response
Finding 14 — Lighting solid angle is a material test variable
Finding 15 — Environment-map prefiltering and material response are coupled
Finding 16 — Clearcoat thickness can affect appearance without changing flake orientation
Finding 17 — Surface defects belong above the clearcoat
Finding 18 — Geometry normals remain part of paint quality
Finding 19 — Colour management can conceal or exaggerate material errors
Finding 20 — Baking is useful only for the spatial parts of the material

CP10-FINAL-001 — What V9 still got wrong
Mistake 1 — The basecoat was still treated too much like a surface colour
Mistake 2 — Process history was described, but not promoted to a first-class shader input
Mistake 3 — Orange peel lacked a full areal-surface statistics framework
Mistake 4 — Geometry quality was still subordinate to material research
Mistake 5 — The camera/display chain was not separated rigorously enough from the material
Mistake 6 — The V9 unified file contained stale package metadata
Mistake 7 — Some advanced Blender features were described before live verification
Mistake 8 — Commercial and community sources need stricter scoping
